# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

# Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7
FROM python:2.7-slim

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the application code to the container
COPY . /app

# Install any required dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Expose the port that your application runs on
EXPOSE 8000

# Command to run the application
CMD ["python", "your_application.py"]
